reviews.review_text: still no performance fix a year after release they took our money and abandoned the game

I'll go ahead and give this game a negative review, too. Did I enjoy it overall? Yeah. It made some decent improvements to the game, and a lot of frankly unnecessary ones, too.

But I cannot recommend this game due to the bugs that are still in this game after release. Bethesda/Virtuous released this game with more bugs than the original, and they have not solved a majority of them over a year after the release. It's completely embarrassing, and another mark on Bethesda's permanent record.

Save yourself the money and go buy something by a developer that deserves your support.

**DOWNLOAD THE OBLIVION R ULTIMATE UNREAL ENGINE.INI FROM NEXUS MODS!!**

Ignore the negative reviews on terrible optimization. While true, there is a method to fix it.

I was getting choppy 40-50 FPS and couldnt play because how horrible it is..after installing the ini I am getting a solid 120fps and it is smooth as hell with no frame generation.

As for the game? It is the oblivion I loved and it just looks amazing. Enjoy

Abandoned game. Recieved its last(?) "update" almost a year ago which somehow made the performance much worse and then nothing since. They basically broke the game for me but Steam wont let me refund since I played more than two hours, even tho the broke it way after that. Nice.
Worth playing for sure, but only if your PC is pretty good. Optimization is really bad and the game required an ini tweak to stop crashing from a vram leak.

Bethesda and Virtuos being what they are. Just pumping and dumping hot slop on gamers and running off with our money laughing at how dumb we are for buying a game that is buggy, stuttery, and just sometimes broken. Yes, visually it's a modern upgrade to a once great game...but read above. I stopped playing it for a whole year hoping for more than the initial small 2 bug fixes last year and nothing since then. Imagine that...they abandoned the game after taking in the millions. Surely gives us all hope Elder Scrolls 6 is going to be a garbage fire.

This is the last time I ever buy anything from Bethesda, adding them to the list with EA, Blizzard, Activision, and Ubisoft. Terrible developers who only care about money and making broken games.

I can't recommend this game. The game itself isn't actually that bad, I think its great. It's pretty congruent with the original with most of the original glitches still there, which proves how hard they tried to keep the game as faithful as possible while porting it over to a new engine.  Yeah, about that last part, the new game engine, Unreal Engine 5 is the problem. How bad is it you ask? I get an average of 60 fps outdoors maxed out, on a 5080. In 1080p. A 4k card is struggling to run an Unreal Engine 5 title in 1080p, now imagine the experience of anyone who didn't blow 2800 dollars for a rig, which according to steam surveys, its most people. Approximately 80% of PC gamers can't even play the game to a standard that's even remotely acceptable in 2026. That's how bad it is.
Abandonware. 1x sneak attack damage bug still plagues bows after over a year. One of the most popular builds in TES doesn't work properly, that tells you how much they care about this game.
These days it's a safer bet to skip Bethesda games. This was patched in 2025 and has been unplayable since.

This game is terribly unoptimized to a level that shouldn't even be allowed in 2026. There's no reason memory issues should be causing as many crashes as this game did. I don't have much playing time on Steam because the game didn't even make it through the compiling shaders or the main menu before crashing.

Oblivion is a great game, held back by some really badly designed systems, and this Remaster fixes those systems and puts an incredibly pretty coat of paint over the whole thing. The biggest drawback for me is the fact that you need a really damn beefy PC to run it - mine runs it fine, but this is pretty much brand new, and my old one did struggle a lot. While I can see the new graphics being a turn-off to some people, I think they’re nice, and the adjustments to gameplay are welcome without, in my opinion, sacrificing the slight jankiness that made it charming. I can see why you might not enjoy this version, but I do enjoy it.

I really wanted to like this remaster, but I can't.

The optimization is horrible, and bugs are frequent.
Other than with graphics, the game feels no different then its predecessors.
Moreover, the developers have completely abandoned the game, meaning these issues will not be fixed.

In its current state, this game should not be bought by anyone.

This game legit killed my PC....
Literally corrupted System32 on my pc and lost the MC world that my friend who died played on. 

I wish I could get my money and mc world back but that's never gonna happen. HOW DO YOU MAKE A GAME THATS SO POORLY OPTIMIZED THAT IT CORRUPTS SYSTEM32 FROM CRASHING SO HARD????????? Please, do NOT get this game and just play the original. Its a great remaster, but the performance alone makes it not worth it. Avoid this game like its a diseased animal, and put it down if you can.

Game is abandoned. The only updates given made the game from good looking but poorly optimized to virtually unplayable. The issues get worse the longer your save file is. Granted, 85 hours is a good amount of playtime for any game- but when an easy substitute already exists in the original version, I cannot see any reason to play this over the og. You can mod it and make the levelling and combat better (as I believe Oblivion by default has the weakest of the three big ES games in terms of a good levelling and balanced rpg system), and you can also make it look better (not Remake levels better, but still improved). 

So yeah, just get og Oblivion and maybe a few mods and skip this remake.
Can’t recommend a game that the developer knowingly releases as garbage, patches a few things, then runs away with all of our money. It seems to be completely abandoned at this point.

Let me say this about the performance, I first started playing it on a rig that had a Ryzen 5 3600x and a 2080 and it was really tough to get through. My PC eventually died on me and I rebuilt it with the current specs below and it ran beautifully other than small stutters in the open world. 

TES IV... Oblivion... what a game to play when there's the urge to immerse yourself in a fantasy world. I first played this game in 2009/10 at 10/11 years old and it was a wonderful experience for younger me to go through. The freedom, the amount of quests and random things you can get sucked into was so fun. I explored every nook and cranny and thought I knew everything but upon revisiting it 15/16 years later, I was wrong. Upon opening the game and getting into the main menu, when that music it was like a wave of nostalgia washed upon me and I felt like I was 10/11 years old again without a care in the world and my whole life ahead of me- it still is but c'mon, being that young versus being 27 now is such a vast difference that revisiting this game really made me feel safe when I was running through quests that I haven't played since the Xbox 360 days. Everything felt familiar but different and it was so surreal seeing how this game looked now in such amazing graphics. 

The quests? Iconic. My favorite one is the hotel boat one... iykyk. The story is really cool but you can definitely finish it in one sitting and I do NOT recommend that. I encourage anyone plaything this to get lost and let the landmarks and whatever your feeling to guide you. What I do is I play a couple of the main quests and then I go and do every faction I possibly can before finishing up the story. Knights of the Nine gets accomplished before the final quest and then the Shivering Isles gets completed after the story as a fun little victory lap. Oblivion definitely shows its age in the combat and mechanics but there's something very charming about playing a 20 year old game that has had a huge impact on my tastes to this day. 

Is it still worth playing? Yes. The original game holds up so well and with a couple mods you can definitely get a comparable experience to this one. Is this one still worth playing? If your PC can handle it, yes. It's literally the same game but with a fresh coat of paint that really looks gorgeous, I found myself looking at scenery and watching the sunset and observing the night sky and just being in awe, comparing these graphics to the OG graphics is literally night and day. 

So, pick this up!! I highly encourage anyone to experience Oblivion and whether you choose OG or Remaster is up to you but the amount of fun to be had in this game is well worth it.

Finally got around to playing this game properly after building a new high end system. It is a stunning remaster that stays true to the original game. However......the constant crashes on load, save file corruptions, shader compilation issues and countless other optimisation problems make the game, largely at times, still unplayable today. Don't waste your money on this if you play on PC. I defend Bethesda as much as I can, but unfortunatley it's another swing and a miss from them with this title.

i was extremely looking forward to this game, between the hype before release and never playing it before. it released damn near unplayable, and i figured after some patches it would be fine. it is now the end of may 2026 and it still is unplayable unless you have a high end pc.. they haven't updated it since july 9th. i will proudly change my review if they fix this hot mess.

this game was abandoned quickly after release and has game breaking bugs that are unfixed a year later still. because of this i cannot progress after 14 hours of gameplay. I bought this game thinking it was an actual remaster but it does not fit the definition. The graphics engine was switched out but i had not realized initially that the actual game engine responsible for how this game plays and feels is the original engine. that is why it is so buggy and even re-introduced bugs that were already fixed by community mods/patches in the original game.

in 15 years on steam this is the one game i regret spending money on the most and i backed star citizen.
This game is utterly broken and unplayable. I would refund it, but I tried for too long to get it to work. Absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

Cant even finish the game. Due to the constant crashes and save file corrupting, tried community mods that help fix this issue because these Dumb devs cant optimize the game properly and even then my game crashes .

I do very much enjoy this remaster, feels like a great way to experience the original game through a new lens. No joke, I cried when I finished the main quest (though likely just due to nostalgia I have for the original)

HOWEVER, The game apparently has REALLY bad performance issues, While I didn't notice anything major, I should mention that for full transparency, the game does have a tendency to crash or run poorly, and to my knowledge there is no plans to fix the game as of now. Something I DID notice, the game has REALLY bad ghosting that is nearly impossible to avoid, and I was playing on Ultra high settings, so I can't Imagine how bad it is on lower end machines.

I will still recommend the game, but ONLY if you have a God PC and pick it up on a sale, Because Oblivion is genuinely one of my favorite Elder Scrolls games and I want more people to experience the game (though maybe it'd be more cost effective to buy the original over the remaster).

Genuinely hope that whenever ES6 roles around in 10 years or whatever they borrow more from this game's roleplaying elements, I'd KILL for a return to form after Skyrim.

Pretty poor quality remake that fails to address most of the original's issues and introduces a swathe of its own. Graphic overhaul is somewhat impressive but ruins the original aesthetic in many places and substitutes it with generic-looking UE assets. With no updates for a very long while, this is clearly a cash-grab which is not worth its asking price. The team behind this clearly had very limited resources and it shows.

Do yourself a favor and play the original instead. There is a million mods made by people way more invested in improving the experience and adding new things, overhauling graphics and gameplay systems.

It is what it is.

I love Oblivion, and actually, I love the remastered version too but there havent been any updates for nearly a year now, even though the game is absolutely riddled with small glitches and bugs. Worst of all are the frequent crashes, which if you dont save often can set you back a long way.

A great opportunity to create a truly perfect remaster was squandered. Anyone who still wishes to buy the game should wait for a sale and not forget to save regularly while playing.
Way over the recommended specs but it still runs like crap, breh. I swear it ran better on release

The remaster runs way worse than the original, and as of writing this, the performance issues were never fixed. Additionally its not as easy to mod as the "traditional engine" used by Bethesda. And while Im glad that the community works on optimizing this game on their own, it shouldnt be their job to put the game in a working condition in the first place.

First and foremost, I intend to continue playing this game because I do enjoy it enough, but this game doesn't deserve the purchases it received as it is just garbage quality wise.  I can see why this is a remaster since they seemed to have only improved the looks of this game while not improving anything significant, leaving a ton of stupid bugs, and adding 0 QoL. Why would you not add the hide helmet feature or let people delete custom spells they make?  There's a lot more head scratching things, but those two kind of give you an idea of what to expect as well as makes clear this was just another cash grab... which can't be too surprising given how much they managed to milk Skyrim while also not really fixing anything.

This game will have its staunch defenders mainly because of how gorgeous the game looks due to Unreal 5 handling that department, but the vast majority of what is good in this game is due to it possessing what was good in the original rather than it being good on its own now.  The fact this game is as old as it is and many things asked for, known, and or modded in through out that time... but they did nothing there?

So, on principal, I can't recommend people buy this game.  Had I known, and or looked into things rather than let my fond memories of the original take over, I wouldn't have purchased this game.

My intro to the Elder Scrolls was Skyrim, which I started playing in 2011. I have spent a LOT of time in Skyrim - it was my happy place and my escape through some... interesting years. 

That's all I've played, these last, what, fifteen years? (okay it's not the only GAME I've played, obviously, but it was the only Elder Scrolls game until I picked up Oblivion Remastered).

The remastered version of Oblivion is thus instantly familiar to me. It's visually comfortably familiar, the UI is familiar, the gameplay is familiar, it feels like coming home.

There are several foundational changes between OG Oblivion and the remaster - so if you've played the original you may want to read up on the differences first. 

It plays well*, it's beautiful, the world is a delight.

The story is pretty decent, I didn't get bored of the main quest like I sort of did in Skyrim (I love skyrim, I do, but I love the side quests if I'm honest.) but there are also lots of other things to keep you occupied.

I hated the Oblivion gates to start with. Found the Oblivion plane labyrinthine and baffling and disorienting. But once you get the gist of how they work, you can get through them more quickly. In fact, to be quite honest, I usually now just bounce my way through, hop over the lava, feel the burn, cheese up the barriers and inclines (did I say I'm used to Skyrim?), into the tower, straight up, bish bash bosh.

The Remastered version comes with all (most of? all??) the DLC and extra content, which includes a few packs with some really overpowered bases. I almost wish they weren't there, or at least that there was a higher barrier to entry for them. To get in, and get Frostcrag Spire with its top tier alchemist garden & table, plus enchanting and spellmaking altars, almost immediately? Feels a bit overpowered. But this has at least pushed me into exploring a Mage type character more than I ever did in Skyrim. 

As soon as I finished the game (well, completed the main questline, all the guilds, and almost finished the Shivering Isles DLC quest line too, before deciding I can enjoy that more another playthrough), I started again with a new character who won't be doing ALL of the things. 

To actually complete the main questline is a rarity for me to be honest - I get bogged down in side quests and collection challenges too easily.

I'm still not bored on my second playthrough. 

I wonder if I should try the original?

* It plays well on my PC, which isn't cutting edge, but is decent. But yes, sometimes it crashes.

Over a year later , Oblivion Remaster still suffers from constant UE5 traversal studder with vastly increases loading times as you progress through the game . Not a single update since the small difficulty update a year ago .

 Bethesda along with Virtuos AGAIN continues to disappoint and prove to everyone they are an overrated studio now that is not competent. A 7.5 billion dollar company can't invest in fixing a game they sold to their loyal customers. They released a broken product , took our money and ran . 

Don't buy this game .

Step. 1) Launch the game
Step 2) FPS in between 20 and 400 on main menu
Step 3) Stutter so hard it's unplayable
Step 4) Close and relaunch game
Step 5) Game runs fine
Step 6) Lose will to live, and go play Crimson Desert instead
Step 7) ???
Step 8) Profit

They updated the game and it went from perfectly playable to unplayable it will boot but i'm getting like 10 fps on the lowest settings where as before the update the game ran fine, i've waited months hoping they'll fix it and they won't going so far as to even put the game on discount etc with no acknowledgment of what seems to be a widespread issue, due to this lack of response and action I feel forced to request a refund for a game I expected to have and play much longer. 

So disappointing, they've lost future business from me.

I was soooo excited for this game, but after a year of leaving the game broken I now HATE Bethesda! I’ve played their games for years but after a mediocre FO4, horrible games like 76 and Starfield, horrible mobile games,  horrible micro transactions and other crash grabs, and the complete mess that FO4 anniversary edition is… BETHESDA IS DEAD TO ME!

Like everyone else said, this is Abandonware. It shouldn't even have a mostly positive in most reviews. The devs gave up on this after a month, after leaving in bugs that's been present for 20 years. The performance is ass, and I have a decently strong PC. Even worse, the stuttering and input lag sucks. 

And if you're reading this, and you think you actually like the remaster. Bethesda doesn't need your protection. Most people who defend this game didn't even beat this game. I did, and it was a waste of time. 

Buy the original and mod it. I love the Last Seed modlist. Go play that.

Also, go check out the Lantern of the laughless saint ad. It's hilarious.

This game was a very pleasant suprise. Had no idea it was coming out then it suddenly appeared bought it on launch and grinded to level 30+ so i can get the best version of all the unique weapons armors etc. After getting level 34  i got a new pc and installed new os . First game i installed on the system was this piece of ♥♥♥♥. I launch the game and my saves are not there i look at the cloud it's empty. A little bit of googling later cloud saves are bugged and not properly saved. Stopped playing for nearly a year. Came back did the whole grind till level 30+ thing i'm at level 26 now. Game crashes every 10 min. It's optimized like ♥♥♥♥. Fast travel crashes the game. Going inside and outside crashes the game. I can understand trying to be loyal to the original but even the original wasnt this buggy.  Give me my 125 hours back you piece of ♥♥♥♥ company. You guys set the lowest standard for the whole game companies. Every time i think that no person with a little bit of shame wouldnt do that you guys and EA come in and say hold my beer. It's been a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ year since this pile of buggy garbage was released, ıt's been nearly 20 years since you guys started releasing buggy games and unfinished products. I hope from the bottom of my heart for you guys to go bankrupt so the decent people in the company can go and work in proper places where their good work is apriciated.

The funny part is the cloud still doesnt ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ work with this piece of ♥♥♥♥ game. Give me my 125+ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hours bethesda.

Long story short buy original oblivion and install fan patches bethesda cant code for ♥♥♥♥. This will  be the last bethesda game i'm buying. Thank god i played starfield on gamepass. I nearly bought it before playing this piece of garbage. Bethesda is the type of company where they can change your mind about their other games while you play a diffrent one.

The lack of updates and staggering levels of incompetence to introduce a bug that renders the game unplayable the more you play it, the unwillingness to fix the issue in any kind of timely manner or acknowledge the issue at all, it's unacceptable. Don't waste your money.
Bethesda is garbage now, don't give them money. Every they touch is ♥♥♥♥. Over a year and no real updates. This game is a scam.

beautiful, but barely functional. it's still oblivion, but with unfixable lag and freezing in the open world. if you're looking to pick up the elder scrolls iv for the first time, definitely choose the original over this version.
5090 users have to roll back their drivers for the game to run. Then after engine tweaks the game still stutters. DEI is always enforced before anti-fraud.

Man I wish they actually went back and fixed the performance issues. Actually went and bought a 9060 xt just for this game. But it still has ghosting and tons of graphical issues. I tried fixing it with settings and then mods, its just not possible to fix on my own. 

Also no matter what gave me a huge headache and made me slightly nauseated while playing. Never have had a game do that before!

This version is trash, but it won't reveal itself as trash until you've made significant progress. If you just want to see Oblivion But Pretty, it's worth about an hour of exploration. If you actually want to get achievements, your save file will certainly break before you achieve anything significant. The original had its own similar problems, but it was more stable than this, and it can be modded to work better than version will ever hope to.

i don't know if it was my pc or the game but the frames kept dropping and didn't have this issue on Skyrim. the games loading screens take a while unlike Skyrim. the combat mechanics are fire, the level up system is great as well as the story but those small mechanical issues with the frames and somewhat long loading screens make it hard to just immerse yourself into the game. i believe Skyrim does everything much better faster loading screens, better frames the combat is still great and the storyline is one of one. the graphics of oblivion are great but its hard to play the game when all of a sudden you screen freezes mid swing and the enemy is one shot and next thing you know youre dead.
didn't fix the original games bugs, added a layer of UE (with bugs), released two updates and abandoned the game.

TLDR: Runs like ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ on every system.

How a remaster should be done. Keeping all the original music, voice actors, broken charm and with minimal quality of life improvements and amazing graphics. Except one enormous problem. It runs like absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ on even the most high-end of systems. It's been over a year and updates have still not fixed anything. Modding supposedly works but that should not be necessary for one of the biggest game developers on the planet. The fact that they opened a discord channel to get feedback on how to improve the game with updates and did absolutley ♥♥♥♥ all with it is a slap in the face. It's a shame because this could have been one of the greatest remasters ever but they did not care about optimizing it at all.

After a year with no patches, I can safely say they’ve unofficially abandoned it. The game still has major performance issues unless you’re running the latest hardware out there, and even then you have to pray you don’t encounter a bug that bricks your save file. The recommended system requirements are honestly a complete lie.

I genuinely can’t recommend it to anyone, even if you find it for $1, because in the end you’ll still run into the same issues — and 1 × 0 is still 0.

I think we should all wait for Skyblivion to release this year. It’ll show just how much better it is, even though it was made by fans, and on top of that, it’ll be free.

Lastly, I’ll say this: Warcraft III: Reforged had a failed launch, but at least the developers still support and update it even six years later. Meanwhile, this troubled remaster was basically abandoned after just one year.

Save your money, and most importantly your time, don’t buy this.

Absolute disgusting performance, looks ♥♥♥♥ while running at 60fps and looks like I'm watching a dream. Looks even more ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ with AMD FSR, which I shouldn't even have to be using. Recommended specs of a 2080 is an absolute joke.

DO NOT BUY THIS GAME

The optimization is awful, with constant stuttering that makes the game literally unplayable, It’s insane. Also somehow the latest patch broke several mods, which is just amazing! It’s like Fallout 4 all over again. I don't even want to bring up the fact that the gameplay feels super outdated, like it’s not 2006 anymore, but that's an entirely different rant.

People need to realize that Bethesda doesn't care about its player base. They will always find new ways to shatter their reputation, or what's left of it.

Fool me once (Starfield), shame on you, fool me twice, shame on me. It's a stuttery mess regardless of your hardware, and it's never gonna be fixed. This is the last time I fall for Todd's sweet lies. Avoid.
They havent updated this since it dropped, what a scam, dont ever support this terrible company.

A masterclass in modern gaming's morally and artistically bankrupt ruling class dumping an unneeded remaster for the masses. Just like many a recent butchering of classic games, the developers have mistaken "realism" (if you can even call it that, as the armor, for example, is completely devoid of practical function or artistic integrity) as a proper substitute for art design, language, and vision. Something as simple as the entrance to the Dark Brotherhood's sanctuary has been desecrated. Compare the low hum and whispers emitting from the door in the original, with its dim surrounding and eerie red glow to the bland, lifeless and overly bright appearance in this version. It is completely devoid of all life and originality seen in the original iteration of Oblivion. On top of that, many textures have not only been downgraded in design for "realism", said textures also appear lazily created, if even done by a human in the first place. Add to this janky animations added during combat, while removing features from the original like dynamic facial expressions during the persuasion mini game, audio mixing done by a deaf person, and performance issues out the ass, I cannot in good conscious recommend this as a standalone game and certainly not as a worthy replacement of the original. Stop supporting low effort attempts at cash grabbing by big studios.
unoptimized = unplayable at times even with a good setup
Im playing multiple Early Access titles atm, and they are ALL more stable than this game.
♥♥♥♥ every single Oblivion fan telling me it's the best game ever when it came out. Just a 20+ year old game with a fresh coat of paint, nothing more.

Fun game. However, the performance is stinky doo doo garbage. This might also be personal preference but I think these unreal engine 5 games, even at high settings, look bad. There is just too much on screen and it hurts my eyes. There also hasn't been a patch for this game since July of last year which probably means many of the performance issues and bugs will never get fixed. Big L, could of been a fun game, would not recommend.
Wanted to play after a year since release and game crashes upon start. I see that other people has this problem as well. They abandoned this game after only 2 patches. Terrible support bethesda.

Oblivion Remastered is a fine game
There is a lot to love:
The Soundtrack is amazing and really sets the mood (Even if some tracks have been memed to death)
Unreal Engine 5 (Regardless of its many issues) gives Oblivion a modern visual uplift
The Main Story was compelling at times with cool characters like Martin Septim and Mankar Camoran
I like the amount of customization you can do with your builds and how doing certain actions like running, jumping, or attacking can evolve as skills and grant new passives and abilities.

There is a lot I don't like too:
The oblivion gates, while cool, are the most boring reused asset slop. Its the same process over and over again. Enter the gate, look for the tower, enter the tower, climb to the orb, and finally take the orb and destroy the portal. There is the occasional deviation like going underground first or jumping over lava, but it will follow this same mundane process over and over again. At a certain point, I would just run past all the enemies to get it over with faster.
Unreal Engine 5 just makes this game rough to play even with my current specs. The micro-stuttering and consistent frame drops from 140 to 64 are playable but it did really bog my immersion. 
Even though I praised the visual uplift of UE5, Oblivion's original aesthetic really clashes with the hyper realistic graphics at times, especially with the characters, in which they will look like aliens or the ugliest things imaginable. The world is also just kind of generic with warm hues and constant dramatic lighting in comparison to the cool ethereal color palette of the original.
Combat is similar to Skyrim with some minor QoL but overall can be mundane and redundant.

I only have 15.2 hours in the game and that was just doing the main quest, some side content, and troubleshooting graphics. I know there is so much more content in the game, not to even mention the dlc, but overall I just can't make my self play the game anymore at this point. I may return to do the guilds and dlc but for now there are other games I am wanting to enjoy. If you are a die hard Elder Scrolls fan than this is nothing less than peak but if you casually enjoyed Skyrim and Fallout 4 then I would save the money and either buy the OG or look for other games.
Seemingly abandoned by Bethesda. With constant crashes, despite effortsm, completely unplayable for me.

Don't bother. Performance is bad and they've abandoned it. 

Plus, there are better mods for the original (which you can still buy, thankfully, unlike what those dogs at Rockstar did).

Also I'm not really a fan of the new voices for some of the NPCs - feels like fan edit

The game doesn't run in an acceptable manner after the latest update and since that was almost a year ago I have zero hopes that they will ever fix the rampant bugs and optimization issues. Don't buy this, not even on a 90% sale. The original game works well enough and that game can actually run on a PC that doesn't cost as much as a car.

The performance is really bad, lots of bugs that have yet to be fixed, games abandoned even though it sold well.

Such a shame, but what could be expected from modern Bethesda, they truly fell off.
Only buy this when it's on sale.  There are still so many bugs and no updates to the game in months.  You might as well just play the original with mods if you really want to return to Oblivion.

Im getting massive stutters on high and medium settings at 4k dlss perfermance settings making the game currently unplayable for me. 
I should have know better than to believe Bethesda or Virtuos would patch and fix the game post lunch to an acceptable standard.

I was extraordinarily excited when I first booted up the remaster and started to play.  Then I hit a wall of frustration with the difficulty quirks and hoped for a patch to fix this.  Got it, great.

So I resumed, only to be hit by another wall regarding performance issues that rendered the game unplayable.  So I modded it with Lumen Begone and the Ultimate Engine Tweaks mod.   Not so great, but okay...I could mod the game to work on my system...for a short while.  Now, the bigger my save file gets, the more I'm hit with insane stuttering and crashing.  I can't walk through an area with heavy foliage without dropping to 30 fps.

Bethesda....come on.  I shouldn't have to mod the game to make it playable in the first place, but even after, I'm seeing a correlation between save file size and performance???  Unacceptable.  100% will not be buying further "remasters" or new releases if you resort to abandoning a game rife with bugs and performance issues.

the game ran fine when it first came out. a few minor bumps, but good enough i suppose if you bought it on sale. It now crashes frequently and incredibly long load times and frame drops. The same thing happened to Fallout 4 and it now requires mods to get it to run correctly. Imagine a world where a company takes your money, nerfs your product for a bunch of unpaid loyalists to fix it for free. What a fall from grace.

The biggest disappointment I've experienced in years. This game runs terribly. I genuinely enjoy the remaster, but I cannot recommend it and plead the developers to OPTIMIZE. Get anyone to do it, modders or 3rd party, whoever. But it must be optimized for Bethesda to feel prideful over this. It is unplayable for most players due to frame drops.

My opinions about this game have since changed.. the lack of mod support is a huge let down and something I was looking forward to. Simply not the same gaming experience without it. Only a very very very small fraction of the replayability and hours of gameplay that original has. The removal of mod support and gentrification of it is a huge hit to Bethesda gaming as a whole. This is just another unoptimized UE5 game.
Runs like ♥♥♥♥, and dont even looks so good, 
Visuals are so generic, all ue5 titles looks now same,  game has no iconic Obivlion vibrant colors, blooms, there is also much less greenery than in the original game, probably because game optimization doesn't exist anyway. so they put lass bushes and grass.
Compared to other modern games, this game doesn't even look good. If you look at the individual assets, they are of high quality, but when you look at the whole, the picture is disappointing.
The game also has a dark or brown filter, which does really clash with the original game visuals.
The characters' faces look strange, and don't look even remotely similar to the original faces, which is theoretically a plus considering how bad the characters' faces look in the original, but I think this remaster failed to leave enough space for the faces to be at least recognizable.

After over a year, this is still a bit of a mess. Any surprise there? I mean, it's had a whole sum of two updates. 

It looks great. When it works. It runs decent. Until it doesn't.

Then, there are the simple, absolute failures. Crash to desktop on save game load. Crash to desktop for "reasons" that aren't quite obvious.  Crashes to desktop for memory leaks. Did I mention crash to desktop yet? Twenty-plus hours in and I have to go back three saves to find an uncorrupted save file.

Bethesda likes to put out buggy games and then let the community fix them. This is exactly what it seems like, folks: A ca$h grab.

I swore off of Bethesda games until well after launch to give the community time to fix what they can't be bothered to fix. Then this came up. Fool me twice, they say. Well, it won't happen a third time.

I love Oblivion and Elder Scrolls but this is genuinely unplayable. It worked somewhat okay at launch but it spikes to 100% CPU utilization right after launch. It doesn't matter what gaming CPU you have, it will use every ounce of processing you can give it and still drop to sub 20 FPS. Modded or unmodded, max settings or minimum, frame gen or no. It is completely unplayable and they seem to have no desire to fix it.

This game is a scam because its unplayable. its 110gb and horribly optimized, stuck between 30-60FPS and can only play for up to 15 minutes before it crashes.. sometimes instantly.

Get the GOTY original oblivion for like $1 which is 10gb and forget this trash. Wish it would let me refund..
